openmeter/billing/rating/service/mutator.purpose: Pre- and post-calculation mutation pipeline that transforms rating.DetailedLines (or PricerCalculateInput) around a pricer run. Each mutator applies one orthogonal pricing concern — min/max spend commitments, credits allocation, percentage discounts, usage-quantity discounts — keeping pricing logic composable and isolated.

patterns.name: Interface segregation by phase
patterns.description: Implement PostCalculationMutator (Mutate(PricerCalculateInput, DetailedLines)->DetailedLines) for output changes, or PreCalculationMutator (Mutate(PricerCalculateInput)->PricerCalculateInput) for input changes. Never conflate the two in one struct.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Every mutator struct declares a blank-identifier assertion against its interface immediately after the type declaration.

patterns.name: ChildUniqueReferenceID for idempotency
patterns.description: Every DetailedLine appended by a mutator sets ChildUniqueReferenceID using rating package constants (rating.MinSpendChildUniqueReferenceID, rating.RateCardDiscountChildUniqueReferenceID) so recalculation is idempotent.

patterns.name: IsLastInPeriod / IsFirstInPeriod guards
patterns.description: Commitment/discount mutators that apply once per billing period guard on i.IsLastInPeriod()/i.IsFirstInPeriod() to avoid double-billing across progressive split lines.

patterns.name: CurrencyCalculator for rounding
patterns.description: All monetary arithmetic uses i.CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision(...) rather than raw alpacadecimal ops, respecting invoice currency precision.

patterns.name: ErrInvoiceLineCreditsNotConsumedFully sentinel
patterns.description: If credits are not fully consumed after iterating all lines, return billing.ErrInvoiceLineCreditsNotConsumedFully — never silently discard the remaining credit value.

patterns.name: MergeDiscountsByChildUniqueReferenceID for usage discount idempotency
patterns.description: DiscountUsage (pre-mutator) calls removeRateCardUsageDiscounts first to strip stale entries, then rewrites via MergeDiscountsByChildUniqueReferenceID so reruns stay idempotent.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the two mutator interfaces (PostCalculationMutator, PreCalculationMutator); all other files implement one.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add methods to either interface without updating every implementor.

key_file_guides.role: MinAmountCommitment and MaxAmountCommitment post-mutators; MinAmount appends a commitment DetailedLine, MaxAmount calls AddDiscountForOverage per line.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MinAmountCommitment reads i.GetPreviouslyBilledAmount(); the new line's period must use i.FullProgressivelyBilledServicePeriod when progressively billed.

key_file_guides.role: Allocates pre-computed CreditsApplied across DetailedLines in order, appending CreditApplied per line; skips non-positive TotalAmount lines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Credits must be fully consumed; any positive remainder triggers ErrInvoiceLineCreditsNotConsumedFully.

key_file_guides.role: Applies a rate-card percentage discount to every line's TotalAmount as an AmountLineDiscountManaged entry.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChildUniqueReferenceID built from rating.RateCardDiscountChildUniqueReferenceID; CorrelationID must be non-empty or the function errors.

key_file_guides.role: Pre-mutator reducing usage quantity per rate-card usage discount via ApplyUsageDiscount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Not idempotent on the raw line — strips old usage discounts before reapplying; PreLinePeriodQuantity must subtract discount already consumed by earlier split lines.

anti_patterns: Adding state to mutator structs — all are stateless value types; context flows through PricerCalculateInput
Skipping ChildUniqueReferenceID on appended DetailedLines — breaks idempotent recalculation
Currency rounding with raw alpacadecimal instead of CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision
Applying min/max commitments outside the IsLastInPeriod guard — double-bills on progressive billing
Implementing both PreCalculationMutator and PostCalculationMutator in one struct

decisions.decision: Two separate interfaces (Pre vs Post) rather than a single pipeline step
decisions.rationale: Pre-mutators change what gets priced (usage quantity); post-mutators change resulting DetailedLines. Conflating forces every mutator to understand both input and output shapes.

decisions.decision: Stateless value-type mutators with no constructors
decisions.rationale: All context is in PricerCalculateInput; stateless types are safe under concurrent calculation and need no DI wiring in app/common.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new PostCalculationMutator that caps line amounts

openmeter/billing/rating/service/rate.purpose: Concrete Pricer implementations — one per productcatalog price type (Flat, Unit, Dynamic, Package, Tiered/Graduated, Tiered/Volume) — plus shared billable-period resolution. Each pricer converts a PricerCalculateInput into rating.DetailedLines and decides whether a line is billable at a given time.

patterns.name: Pricer interface with compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every pricer satisfies Pricer (GenerateDetailedLines + ResolveBillablePeriod) and declares var _ Pricer = (*MyPricer)(nil) after the type.

patterns.name: Embed base pricers for period resolution
patterns.description: Usage-based pricers supporting progressive billing embed ProgressiveBillingMeteredPricer; pricers that never split embed NonProgressiveBillingPricer. Do not reimplement ResolveBillablePeriod.

patterns.name: ChildUniqueReferenceID constants from rating package
patterns.description: All DetailedLine entries set ChildUniqueReferenceID using rating package constants, never raw string literals.

patterns.name: IsLastInPeriod / IsFirstInPeriod payment-term gating
patterns.description: InAdvance lines emit only when l.IsFirstInPeriod(); InArrears lines only when l.IsLastInPeriod() (or always for per-usage lines), preventing duplicate billing across split lines.

patterns.name: TieredPriceCalculator callback for graduated tier math
patterns.description: Graduated tiered math uses GraduatedTiered.TieredPriceCalculator with a TierCallbackFn receiving TierCallbackInput per tier boundary — do not reimplement range-splitting inline.
patterns.example: p.TieredPriceCalculator(TieredPriceCalculatorInput{TieredPrice: price, FromQty: usage.PreLinePeriodQuantity, ToQty: ..., TierCallbackFn: func(in TierCallbackInput) error {...}})

patterns.name: billing.ValidationError for invariant violations
patterns.description: Return billing.ValidationError{Err: ...} (not fmt.Errorf) when a configuration violates a billing invariant such as progressive billing on a flat-price line.

patterns.name: Tiered struct as mode router
patterns.description: Tiered inspects price.Mode and delegates to volume or graduated sub-pricer; new modes must be added to both switch blocks (GenerateDetailedLines and ResolveBillablePeriod).

key_file_guides.role: Pricer interface and PricerCalculateInput (embeds StandardLineAccessor + CurrencyCalculator + Usage + StandardLineDiscounts); IsLastInPeriod/IsFirstInPeriod helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Usage is a pointer; call GetUsage() for a safe copy — direct dereference panics when Usage is nil.

key_file_guides.role: ProgressiveBillingMeteredPricer and NonProgressiveBillingPricer providing shared ResolveBillablePeriod; progressive truncates asOf to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NonProgressiveBillingPricer rejects lines with a SplitLineGroupID; embedding it blocks progressive billing for that price type.

key_file_guides.role: GraduatedTiered and TieredPriceCalculator; splitTierRangeAtBoundary handles FromQty/ToQty cutting across tier boundaries for progressive billing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TieredPriceCalculatorInput.Validate() must pass; FromQty and ToQty non-negative and FromQty <= ToQty.

key_file_guides.role: Router dispatching to volume/graduated by price.Mode — the only external entry point for tiered pricing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new tiered mode requires updates to both switch blocks (GenerateDetailedLines and ResolveBillablePeriod).

key_file_guides.role: FlatPrice pricer with custom ResolveBillablePeriod using line.InvoiceAt instead of period end; no progressive billing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Compares invoiceAt vs asOf (both truncated); InAdvance emits only on IsFirstInPeriod, InArrears only on IsLastInPeriod.

anti_patterns: Reimplementing tier-boundary range splitting outside TieredPriceCalculator
Returning fmt.Errorf for billing invariant violations — use billing.ValidationError{Err: ...}
Setting ChildUniqueReferenceID as a raw string literal instead of rating constants
Adding progressive-billing support to a pricer embedding NonProgressiveBillingPricer without switching the base type
Calling PricerCalculateInput.Usage directly instead of GetUsage() — panics when Usage is nil

decisions.decision: Separate struct per price type rather than a single switch-heavy function
decisions.rationale: Each price type has distinct period-resolution semantics and line structure; separate structs allow independent testing and embedding the correct base pricer.

decisions.decision: TieredPriceCalculator as a reusable callback-driven algorithm
decisions.rationale: Graduated tiered pricing needs range splitting across FromQty/ToQty boundaries; centralising with TierCallbackFn avoids duplicating the range logic in both volume and graduated pricers.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new usage-based pricer for a 'stepped' price type

openmeter/billing/rating/service/testuti.purpose: Test harness for pricer and mutator unit tests. Provides CalculationTestCase, line-mode factories, and RunCalculationTestCase which wires a real service.New() and compares JSON-serialised DetailedLines output.

patterns.name: RunCalculationTestCase as the single test entry point
patterns.description: All pricer/mutator tests call testutil.RunCalculationTestCase(t, CalculationTestCase{...}) — never instantiate service.New() directly in test files.
patterns.example: testutil.RunCalculationTestCase(t, testutil.CalculationTestCase{Price: ..., LineMode: testutil.SinglePerPeriodLineMode, Usage: ..., Expect: ...})

patterns.name: TestLineMode enum for split-line scenarios
patterns.description: Use TestLineMode constants (SinglePerPeriodLineMode, MidPeriodSplitLineMode, LastInPeriodSplitLineMode) to control whether the test line has a SplitLineGroupID and how its period sits within TestFullPeriod.

patterns.name: JSON equality assertion for DetailedLines
patterns.description: RunCalculationTestCase uses require.JSONEq on marshalled slices to avoid nil-vs-empty-slice false failures and give readable diffs. Do not require.Equal DetailedLines slices directly.

patterns.name: PreviousBilledAmount for progressive billing tests
patterns.description: Set CalculationTestCase.PreviousBilledAmount to simulate already-billed amounts; the harness inserts a fake prior line with that Totals.Amount.

key_file_guides.role: Single file defining CalculationTestCase, FeatureUsageResponse, TestLineMode, TestFullPeriod, and RunCalculationTestCase (imports service.New() to run a real pipeline).
key_file_guides.watch_for: The harness sets line name to 'feature'; expected DetailedLine.Name values must match that prefix (e.g. 'feature: usage in period', 'feature: minimum spend').

anti_patterns: Instantiating service.New() directly in *_test.go files — use RunCalculationTestCase
Constructing billing.StandardLine manually in test files outside this package — use the harness to avoid missing SplitLineHierarchy setup
Using require.Equal for DetailedLines slices — RunCalculationTestCase normalises nil/empty via JSON equality
Adding test helper state to ubptest.go beyond CalculationTestCase fields — use CalculationTestCase.Options for pricer-level overrides

decisions.decision: JSON equality rather than deep-equal for DetailedLines
decisions.rationale: DetailedLines holds alpacadecimal.Decimal fields whose zero values differ between nil and empty; JSON normalises these and gives legible diffs.
